title: Firm Resource Adequacy Standard
text: An Act concerning resource adequacy. 'Qualifying capacity resource' means a generation resource capable of firm, dispatchable, around-the-clock output with zero direct carbon dioxide emissions. Beginning in 2029, load-serving entities shall procure sufficient qualifying capacity resources to meet a firm reserve margin established by the commission.
note: HAND-AUTHORED. Indirect inclusion via firm/dispatchable/zero-carbon capacity definition.

title: Renewable Energy Acceleration Act
text: An Act to accelerate renewable energy. 'Renewable energy resource' means solar, wind, geothermal, and small hydroelectric generation. Each electric utility shall procure no less than eighty percent of retail load from renewable energy resources by 2035.
note: HAND-AUTHORED. Indirect exclusion: closed renewable list omits nuclear despite a clean-energy framing.

title: Nuclear Plant Preservation Credit Act
text: An Act relating to electricity generation. The commission shall establish a zero-emission credit program to prevent the premature retirement of existing nuclear generating units that provide carbon-free baseload power. Eligible units may receive credits for each megawatt-hour generated.
note: HAND-AUTHORED. Direct ZEC fleet preservation; says 'nuclear' so not indirect.

title: Nuclear Moratorium Repeal Act
text: An Act relating to nuclear power. The General Assembly hereby enacts a repeal of the prohibition on the construction of nuclear power facilities, removing the requirement that a permanent federal waste repository exist before the commission may approve a new reactor.
note: HAND-AUTHORED. Direct: repeal of a ban -> helps. Strategically opens the state but economically diffuse at introduction -> medium.

title: Data Center Co-Location Enablement Act
text: An Act relating to large electric loads. A large load exceeding one hundred megawatts may co-locate with an electric generation facility and take service behind the meter. Bring-your-own-generation arrangements are permitted, and the commission shall establish streamlined interconnection standards for such configurations.
note: HAND-AUTHORED. Indirect, ENABLING co-location -> helps (contrast with PA SB 77 which restricts).

title: Highway Infrastructure Funding Act
text: An Act relating to transportation. The Department of Transportation shall allocate funds for the resurfacing of state highways, replacement of structurally deficient bridges, and expansion of public transit corridors in metropolitan planning areas.

title: Radiopharmaceutical Supply Chain Act
text: An Act relating to medical isotopes. The Department of State Health Services shall license facilities producing radiopharmaceuticals and medical radioisotopes for nuclear medicine, and establish chain-of-custody protocols for diagnostic nuclear imaging agents in clinical settings.
note: HAND-AUTHORED. Hard negative: contains 'nuclear' but medical, out of scope.

title: Fusion Energy Research Grant Act
text: An Act relating to fusion energy. The state shall establish a competitive grant program supporting university research into fusion energy and magnetic-confinement tokamak demonstration projects, with awards administered by the Department of Commerce.
note: HAND-AUTHORED. Out of scope: fusion, not commercial fission.

title: Integrated Resource Planning Reform Act
text: An Act concerning utility planning. In each integrated resource plan, an electric utility shall identify a least-cost portfolio and shall expressly consider firm, dispatchable, zero-carbon resources capable of providing baseload reliability. The commission shall evaluate cost recovery and prudency for resources selected in the approved plan.
note: HAND-AUTHORED. Indirect: IRP statute that explicitly contemplates firm zero-carbon resources -> helps.

title: Carbon-Free Electricity Standard
text: An Act establishing a carbon-free electricity standard. 'Carbon-free resource' means wind, solar photovoltaic, and qualifying battery storage. The commission shall not approve cost recovery for any new thermal generating unit. By 2040 all load-serving entities shall serve retail load exclusively with carbon-free resources.
note: HAND-AUTHORED. Adversarial: labeled 'carbon-free' but the closed list excludes nuclear AND bars thermal cost recovery (nuclear is steam-cycle thermal).
